name: solemn cigarette
content: it's a thin, papery feeling; letting-
go. yellow tip, sun setting,
turning grey, just like-
they said I would.
a thick red, then that white swell,
and then that smell. it's a familiar face,
a race to the end, to the butt,
but-
it's slow, that burn, that anchor-
of the sun, on every fibre-
of my lungs. it's a lost cause,
a rat's flight-
up a wall. fight-
the urge, drown the night-
out with your lights,
or golds, or blacks, but don't-
stop dead in your tracks.
it's a fraction-
of a second, 'fore the white turns-
to ash, like some blinding flash, like-
some distant paradise, that fools-
my eyes. they're black, but they're-
a little red too. a little-
bed-
for two, for you, for me.
